{{Infobox construct
| name = Nova-Bots
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Nova-Bots Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Nova-Bots are versatile robotic entities that vary significantly in scale, ranging from microscopic particles and finger-sized swarms to massive units capable of carrying fighters or functioning as heavy shields. While they are generally humanoid in shape, their physical configurations are highly diverse; some possess multiple bladed arms, others feature specialized tools like fabricators or cannons on their limbs, and some are designed as squat drones with jetpacks. They can be constructed from skeletal frames using wiring, circuitry, and armor, or even integrated with synth-flesh. A signature characteristic of many Nova-Bots is a single glowing eye, which typically appears red as a mark of [[Doctor Nova]]'s design, though many others feature purple eyes. They serve various roles including combatants, pilots for dark-metal bird-like spacecraft, laborers for construction and repairs, medical assistants, and even disposable shields used to protect living soldiers. They are highly reliable and can be mass-produced, with some groups functioning as entire crews for ships or acting as a regimented cloud in battle. Some advanced versions are even capable of being directly controlled or used as decoys.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Nova-Bots]] are small brass humanoids.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c15">''The Captain'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Mell]] has a Nova-Bot that is a tiny brass humanoid.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Associated with [[Doctor Nova]], these robots vary significantly in size from tiny, hand-sized units to massive entities equipped with shoulder-mounted hologram projectors. While they are generally human in shape with a variable number of limbs and eyes that glow purple or blazing red, they can also function as a cloud of micro-bots used to infect weapons. These entities assemble themselves into legions and perform various tasks, such as stitching a ship together, sweeping systems for debris, or acting as decoys for [[Mell]]. Some models are the size of children with fabricators instead of hands, while others can be equipped with guns. During a confrontation, the robots streamed into a room and filled the hallways with light before [[Starhammer]] blew them apart.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] are giant robots used by [[Doctor Nova]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c6">''The Engineer'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] are robots associated with [[Doctor Nova]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]]'s [[Nova-Bots]] are executed by the narrator.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c15">''The Engineer'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|A hand-sized Nova-Bot exists.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c20">''The Engineer'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] are generally human in shape with a variable number of limbs.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24">''The Engineer'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] typically feature a single blazing red eye.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] are entities that assemble one another in a legion.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|A Nova-Bot is a small robot.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c29">''The Engineer'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]] works with [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] are the size of human children and have fabricators on the end of their arms in place of hands.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] are responsible for stitching the ship together.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] are sweeping the system of debris.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c39">''The Engineer'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|A tiny Nova-Bot provided [[Mell]] with a comforting pat.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41">''The Engineer'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] streamed into the room with [[Starhammer]] and [[Leilari]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c46">''The Engineer'', chapter 46</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] can be equipped with guns.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47">''The Engineer'', chapter 47</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] can be used as a cloud of micro-bots to infect weapons.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] can be reassigned to perform repairs.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Nova-Bots]] fill hallways with purple light from their eyes.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator lost sight of the Nova-Bot while being tied into place.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c49">''The Engineer'', chapter 49</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Nova Bots have purple eyes.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52">''The Engineer'', chapter 52</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|A massive Nova-Bot has bulky devices on its shoulders that project holograms.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Starhammer]] blew the [[Nova-Bots]] apart.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c52" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Mell]] uses [[Nova-Bots]] as decoys.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c53">''The Engineer'', chapter 53</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|These smooth, dark metal machines are vaguely bird-shaped craft equipped with jetpacks that possess the ability to explode into molten metal. Described by Shyrax as reliable, these units are printed by [[Mell]]'s team and can be used to watch halls or serve as doctors, such as one featuring white flared armor and a single purple eye. [[Mell]] utilizes these bots alongside a robotic avatar, sometimes using one as a flashlight or having them hold boxes and disable nearby ships. While [[Horizon]] suggests giving a unit to [[Ark]], [[Mell]] maintains control by transferring data and components into the fifteen bots under her command.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A Nova-Bot is a robot that can explode into molten metal.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c4">''The Knight'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Nova-Bot crews are described as reliable by Shyrax.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c5">''The Knight'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Nova-Bots]] are being printed by [[Mell]]'s team.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17">''The Knight'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mell]] cowered behind two larger [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c18">''The Knight'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A Nova-Bot held a box for [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c22">''The Knight'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A Nova-Bot is something [[Horizon]] suggests giving to [[Ark]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c23">''The Knight'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mell]] carries a glowing Nova-Bot used like a flashlight.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c28">''The Knight'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|A Nova-Bot with white flared armor and a single purple eye acts as a doctor.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c32">''The Knight'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Nova-Bots]] are used to watch the halls.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c33">''The Knight'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mell]] has fifteen [[Nova-Bots]] that can disable nearby ships.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36">''The Knight'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mell]] utilized [[Nova-Bots]] and a robotic avatar.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c37">''The Knight'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Nova-Bots]] are smooth, vaguely bird-shaped craft of dark metal.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Nova-Bots]] are equipped with jetpacks.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c40">''The Knight'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Mell]] can transfer components/data into a Nova-Bot.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42">''The Knight'', chapter 42</ref>}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Nova-Bots are silvery, humanoid androids featuring single glowing purple eyes and the ability to transform their hands into lasers. These mass-produced units serve as automated hangar crews, shipment loggers, and pilots for [[Karoshan ships]], though an entire army of them was once noted as missing from the [[Zenith]] Starship. Coordinated by [[Mell]], who requires them to function and even incorporates their metal frame into her own face, these robots can specialize in cracking network encryption or collecting biological samples. [[Mell]] maintains the units by installing data cores and assembling new models, though a connection to the Nova-Bots was eventually lost. During their service, these machines were even capable of seizing the [[Zenith Blade]].}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] are small robots used by [[Mell]] for piloting and combat.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c3">''The Pilot'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell]]'s face is partially composed of a Nova-Bot metal frame.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c4">''The Pilot'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] can specialize in cracking network encryption.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell]] requires [[Nova-Bots]] to function.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14">''The Pilot'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] are mass-produced pilots for [[Karoshan ships]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] are coordinated by [[Mell]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] seized the [[Zenith Blade]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c15">''The Pilot'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] are automated crews in the hangar.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c18">''The Pilot'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell]] is assembling a Nova-Bot.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c19">''The Pilot'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] are silvery humanoid robots that can transform their hands into lasers.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mell]] installs data cores into [[Nova-Bots]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Zenith Starship]]'s army of [[Nova-Bots]] is missing.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25">''The Pilot'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] are generally humanoid androids with single eyes that glow purple.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] can be used to take biological samples.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c30">''The Pilot'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Nova-Bots]] can be used to log shipments.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c40">''The Pilot'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The connection to the [[Nova-Bots]] was lost.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c49">''The Pilot'', chapter 49</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|“We’re printing ships and Nova-Bots as fast as we can,” Mell went on<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|"I have fifteen Nova-Bots I think can make it to the closest ships and disable them.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c36" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|"I can get that into a Nova-Bot, I know I can, but you� have to help me with the magical bits."<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c42" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“I can’t do anything without Nova-Bots!” Mell cried.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c14" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Constructs]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
